headline: USA RARE EARTH DROPS $2.8B to Control Pela Ema — The Only Non-Asian Source of All Four Magnetic Rare Earths
snippet: USA Rare Earth (USAR) signed a definitive agreement to acquire Serra Verde Group for ~$2.8B in cash and stock, securing Brazil's Pela Ema mine — the only ionic clay operation outside Asia capable of supplying all four magnetic rare earths at commercial scale, backed by a $565M DFC financing package and a 15-year offtake agreement.

headline: Q2 METALS POSTS 295 Mt Cisco Lithium Resource — 9.9 Mt LCE Among Americas' Largest Hard-Rock Deposits
snippet: Q2 Metals announced an inaugural inferred mineral resource of 295 million tonnes grading 1.36% Li₂O (9.9 Mt LCE) at its Cisco Lithium Project in Quebec's James Bay region — based on 75 drill holes totalling 33,343m — ranking among the largest hard-rock lithium deposits in the Americas.

headline: NEW FOUND GOLD Expands Dropkick to 1.4 km Strike — 24.8 g/t Au over 14m, Zone Still Open in Multiple Directions
snippet: New Found Gold released final 2025 drill results from the Dropkick Zone at its Queensway Gold Project (Newfoundland), highlighting 24.8 g/t Au over 14.0m and 13.4 g/t Au over 8.1m; the mineralized trend has now been extended to over 1.4 km strike and ~300m depth with the zone remaining open as the 2026 program gets underway with four rigs active.

headline: FORTUNE BAY Steps 135m Below Box Deposit — Drills 9.9 g/t Au over 7m in Largest-Ever Goldfields Step-Out
snippet: Fortune Bay Corp (FOR.V) released results from hole B26-350, a 135m step-out below the existing Box Deposit at its Goldfields Gold Project (Saskatchewan), returning 3.7 g/t Au over 21.0m including 9.9 g/t Au over 7.0m — confirming the mineralizing system extends significantly below the current resource footprint.

headline: F3 URANIUM Hits Tetra Zone in 3 of 6 Step-Out Holes at Patterson Lake North — Athabasca Basin Zone Expands
snippet: F3 Uranium Corp (FUU.V) announced winter 2026 diamond drilling results from the Tetra Zone on its Patterson Lake North Project in Saskatchewan's Athabasca Basin, intersecting uranium mineralization in three of six step-out holes testing strike and plunge extensions of a zone that previously returned 13.0m of 0.28% U3O8.

headline: COPPER SURGES to $6.10/lb — Two-Month High on Ceasefire Bets, But Smelter Margin Collapse Flags Concentrate Crunch
snippet: Copper futures climbed to ~$6.10/lb Wednesday, fully recovering losses since the start of the US-Iran conflict as ceasefire extension talks lifted base metals; however, smelter treatment charges have collapsed to multi-year lows, signalling tightening concentrate supply that analysts warn will keep a floor under long-term copper prices regardless of geopolitical noise.

headline: GOLD RECOVERS to $4,752 as Iran Ceasefire Extended — Metal Sits 10% Below Conflict Peak, Technical Re-Entry Eyed
snippet: Gold recovered to $4,752/oz Wednesday after President Trump extended the US-Iran ceasefire, though bullion remains nearly 10% below its conflict-driven peak; silver held at $76.84/oz as traders weighed whether a second round of US-Iran talks before the ceasefire's expiry marks a durable de-escalation or just another pause.

headline: WELLS FARGO: We're Only Halfway Through the 4th Debasement Cycle — Gold Bull Case Pushes to $8,000 by 2027
snippet: Wells Fargo Securities argues the 'debasement trade' is in a fourth cycle (since 2022) that averages 8.5 years — meaning it's only 3.5 years in; the bank's base case for gold is $6,000–$6,300/oz in 2026, with a bull scenario reaching $8,000 by 2027 as four of five economic scenarios point to continued fiat currency debasement.

headline: CHINA IMPORTS 790+ TONNES of Silver in Early 2026 — Fifth Straight Deficit Year as Solar Demand Accelerates
snippet: GoldSeek's weekly SWOT analysis reports China imported over 790 tonnes of silver in early 2026 — driven by solar panel manufacturing — while the market records its fifth consecutive annual supply deficit; the gold-to-silver ratio has contracted from above 105, a historically bullish signal analysts say has preceded sharp silver outperformance in prior cycles.

headline: WEDBUSH PROJECTS $90 for MP MATERIALS — Is Mountain Pass the Only Investable Rare Earth Independence Play?
snippet: Wedbush Securities raised its price target on MP Materials (MP) to $90, citing Mountain Pass as the only scaled rare earth mining and processing facility in the Western Hemisphere; the note questions whether MP is the lone investable pure-play on US rare earth independence as Project Vault deploys $10B in government-backed financing.
